title: Sarga 84
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “After Rama and the great-souled Bharata had spoken, Lakshmana then addressed Rama, the delight of the Raghus, with auspicious words.” and develops toward “O mighty-armed one, Vritra, who is performing penance, has conquered the worlds; he is indeed powerful and righteous, and I am unable to control him.”

This passage begins with “O Lord of Asuras, if he performs penance again, the worlds will remain subservient to him as long as they endure.” and develops toward “Indeed, you have always rendered help to these great-souled ones, for this task is unbearable for others, and you are the sole refuge for the…”

verses.sanskrit: तथोक्तवति रामे तु भरते च महात्मनि । लक्ष्मणो ऽथ शुभं वाक्यमुवाच रघुनन्दनम् ।। 7.84.1 ।।
verses.translation: After Rama and the great-souled Bharata had spoken, Lakshmana then addressed Rama, the delight of the Raghus, with auspicious words.

verses.sanskrit: अश्वमेधो महायज्ञः पावनः सर्वपाप्मनाम् । पावनस्तव दुर्धर्षो रोचतां रघुनन्दन ।। 7.84.2 ।।
verses.translation: O delight of the Raghus, may the Ashwamedha, a great sacrifice and purifier of all sins, which is a purifier for you, the unconquerable one, be pleasing to you.

verses.sanskrit: श्रूयते हि पुरावृत्तं वासवे सुमहात्मनि । ब्रह्महत्यावृतः शक्रो हयमेधेन पावितः ।। 7.84.3 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, it is heard that in an ancient event, the very great-souled Indra, afflicted by the sin of Brahman-slaughter, was purified by the Ashwamedha sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: पुरा किल महाबाहो देवासुरसमागमे । वृत्रो नाम महानासीद्दैतेयो लोकसम्मतः ।। 7.84.4 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed one, it is said that formerly, during the conflict between gods and asuras, there was a great Daitya named Vritra, renowned throughout the world.

verses.sanskrit: विस्तीर्णो योजनशतमुच्छ्रितस्त्रिगुणं ततः । अनुरागेण लोकांस्त्रीन्स्नेहात्पश्यति सर्वतः ।। 7.84.5 ।।
verses.translation: He was a hundred yojanas wide and three times that in height, and he regarded all three worlds everywhere with affection and love.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मज्ञश्च कृतज्ञश्च बुद्ध्या च परिनिष्ठितः । शशास पृथिवीं स्फीतां धर्मेण सुसमाहितः ।। 7.84.6 ।।
verses.translation: He, being righteous, grateful, and perfected in intellect, ruled the vast and prosperous earth with perfect attention to dharma.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्प्रशासति तदा सर्वकामदुघा मही । रसवन्ति प्रभूनानि मूलानि च फलानि च ।। 7.84.7 ।।
verses.translation: While he was ruling, the earth became a wish-fulfilling cow, providing abundant and juicy roots and fruits.

verses.sanskrit: अकृष्टपच्या पृथिवी सुसम्पन्ना महात्मनः । स राज्यं तादृशं भुङ्क्ते स्फीतमद्भुतदर्शनम् ।। 7.84.8 ।।
verses.translation: The earth, without cultivation, yielded abundant crops for that great-souled king, and he enjoyed such a prosperous and marvelous kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य बुद्धिः समुप्तन्ना तपः कुर्यामनुत्तमम् । तपो हि परमं श्रेयः सम्मोहमितरत्सुखम् ।। 7.84.9 ।।
verses.translation: A thought arose in his mind that he should perform unsurpassed penance, for indeed, penance is the supreme good, while other forms of happiness lead to delusion.

verses.sanskrit: स निक्षिप्य सुतं ज्येष्ठं पौरेषु मधुरेश्वरम् । तप उग्रं समातिष्ठत्तापयन्सर्वदेवताः ।। 7.84.10 ।।
verses.translation: He entrusted his eldest son, Madhureśvara, to the citizens and undertook severe penance, thereby causing all the deities to suffer.

verses.sanskrit: तपस्तप्यति वृत्रे तु वासवः परमार्तवत् । विष्णुं समुपसङ्क्रम्य वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ।। 7.84.11 ।।
verses.translation: When Vritra was performing penance, Indra, greatly distressed, approached Vishnu and spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: तपस्यता महाबाहो लोकाः वृत्रेण निर्जिताः । बलवान्स हि धर्मात्मा नैनं शक्ष्यामि शासितुम् ।। 7.84.12 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed one, Vritra, who is performing penance, has conquered the worlds; he is indeed powerful and righteous, and I am unable to control him.

verses.sanskrit: यद्यसौ तप आतिष्ठेद्भूय एवासुरेश्वर । यावल्लोका धरिष्यन्ति तावदस्य वशानुगाः ।। 7.84.13 ।।
verses.translation: O Lord of Asuras, if he performs penance again, the worlds will remain subservient to him as long as they endure.

verses.sanskrit: तं चैनं परमोदारमुपेक्षसि महाबलम् । क्षणं हि न भवेद्वृत्रः क्रुद्धे त्वयि सुरेश्वर ।। 7.84.14 ।।
verses.translation: And yet you disregard this extremely powerful Vritra; for indeed, Vritra would not exist even for a moment if you, O Lord of gods, were to become angry.

verses.sanskrit: यदा हि प्रीतिसंयोगं त्वया विष्णो समागतः । तदाप्रभृति लोकानां नाथत्वमुपलब्धवान् ।। 7.84.15 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, it was when he obtained your favor, O Vishnu, that he achieved lordship over the worlds from that time onwards.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं प्रसादं लोकानां कुरुष्व सुसमाहितः । त्वत्कृतेन हि सर्वं स्याप्रशान्तमरुजं जगत् ।। 7.84.16 ।।
verses.translation: You, being well-composed, should bestow your favor upon the worlds, for by your action alone, the entire world would become peaceful and free from suffering.

verses.sanskrit: इमे हि सर्वे विष्णो त्वां निरीक्षन्ते दिवौकसः । वृत्रघातेन महता तेषां साह्यं कुरुष्व ह ।। 7.84.17 ।।
verses.translation: O Vishnu, all these gods indeed await you; therefore, render them great help by slaying Vritra.

verses.sanskrit: त्वया हि नित्यशः साह्यं कृतमेषां महात्मनाम् । असह्यमिदमन्येषामगतीनां गतिर्भवान् ।। 7.84.18 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, you have always rendered help to these great-souled ones, for this task is unbearable for others, and you are the sole refuge for the helpless. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे चतुरशीतितमः सर्गः ।। 84 ।। Thus ends the eighty-fourth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
